body_txt: Blinded by Rage is when a fighter actively tries to make their opponent angry, because people can't fight or perform as well when they're mad. Usually, as someone gets more angry in a fight, their attacks gain strength and speed at the expense of technique and accuracy. More mistakes get made as the fighter gets sloppy, reckless, or tunnel-visioned due to their anger clouding their judgment. This allows their opponent to gain an advantage in several ways — they could lead the raging fighter into a trap , get them to hit something they really need to avoid hitting , take advantage of the aforesaid worse technique that leaves the raging fighter wide open when they miss, or cause them to waste ammo or stamina in their fury and press the advantage when they can't fight back anymore. Other advantages to making your opponent angry include keeping their focus on you while your allies sneak up behind your opponent for a knife In the Back , sabotaging the Evil Plan , or making them blurt out something that they wouldn't say while they were calm . This can also work to set up a Heroic Sacrifice , as in cases where the angry opponent is far stronger than the heroes, someone may opt to keep them busy at the expense of their own survival, while the others make it past with their lives intact. A common weakness for The Berserker , where blind rage proves to be a flaw in their fighting style. May also be caused by a Hate Plague . Characters might also start speaking in Angrish . A Sister Trope to Revenge Before Reason . A subversion of Unstoppable Rage and You Wouldn't Like Me When I'm Angry! . See also Berserk Button , Trash Talk , I Shall Taunt You , and You Fight Like a Cow , which is often meant to provoke this reaction, Villainous Breakdown , when the villains typically lose their composure right before their defeat, Rage Judo , another way to use someone's anger to your advantage, and Moment of Weakness , where getting angry causes a dramatic lapse in judgment. Fury-Fueled Foolishness is similar to this, but without the "intentionally invoked by someone else" angle. In non-physical contexts, this might result in a Misdirected Outburst . Contrast Tranquil Fury when angering someone makes him more focused and calm instead, and Nobody Calls Me "Chicken"! , for when you want them angry so they can show off better. Also contrast Temperate Berserker , where they perform just as well regardless of their rage. People who try to get this reaction out of others include the Warrior Therapist (evil version, specifically), the Batman Gambitter , and the Troll .
